title: Java Streams API
content:
### Streams API

The Stream API (Java 8+) provides a functional approach to process sequences of elements.

---

### Creating Streams

```java
list.stream()                           // from collection
Arrays.stream(array)                     // from array
Stream.of(1, 2, 3)                      // from values
Files.lines(Paths.get("file.txt"))      // from file
Stream.iterate(0, n -> n + 1)           // infinite stream
```

---

### Intermediate Operations

| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `filter(predicate)` | Keep elements matching predicate |
| `map(function)` | Transform each element |
| `flatMap(function)` | Flatten nested streams |
| `sorted()` | Sort elements |
| `distinct()` | Remove duplicates |
| `limit(n)` | Take first n elements |
| `skip(n)` | Skip first n elements |
| `peek(consumer)` | Debug each element |

---

### Terminal Operations

| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `forEach(consumer)` | Process each element |
| `collect(collector)` | Collect to collection |
| `toList()` | Collect to list |
| `count()` | Count elements |
| `anyMatch(predicate)` | Any match? |
| `allMatch(predicate)` | All match? |
| `noneMatch(predicate)` | None match? |
| `findFirst()` | First element |
| `reduce(binaryOp)` | Reduce to single value |
| `min(comparator)` | Minimum element |
| `max(comparator)` | Maximum element |

---

### Stream Pipeline

```java
list.stream()
    .filter(s -> s.length() > 3)
    .map(String::toUpperCase)
    .sorted()
    .collect(Collectors.toList());
```

> **Key Point:** Streams are lazy — intermediate operations are not executed until a terminal operation is called. Streams cannot be reused after a terminal operation. Use parallel streams for performance on large datasets.

quiz.question: Are streams reusable after a terminal operation?
What triggers execution of intermediate operations?
